title: dryopteris filix-mas subsp. patentissima
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. patentissima, also known as the upright male fern, is a subspecies of the male fern (Dryopteris filix-mas), a type of perennial fern native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The upright male fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. This plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the upright male fern can be used in teas, infusions, or as a component in tinctures, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
